text:
On a summer's afternoon, in a quiet suburban park away from it all, there was a small grove filled with wildflowers. Among these blossoms, a hummingbird soared with remarkable grace. The bird was on an odyssey of the sweetest kind—-seeking the legendary Double Wide Stout. This particular batch was said to have flavors reminiscent of old barnyard olives mixed with wild raspberry swirls.

The hummingbird glanced down at its tiny feet dangling from its feathers. Every beat sent tiny leaves billowing around it. It wouldn't have such tantalizing snacks year-round. Its only chance was early morning. It hovered just above a bush, collecting tiny dewdrops.

Just as it had almost convinced itself it wouldn't make it, a shadow loomed. A young girl with shining eyes stumbled into the glade. Her name was Lily. She loved nature, especially hummingbirds.

"Good morning!" she said excitedly. The hummingbird swooped down. She leaned forward tentatively
